{"key_event":"Montt consolidation and expanding liberal opposition","reasoning":"The Portalian state remained strong and became more legally and territorially capable through military victory in 1851, infrastructure, southern colonization, and the Civil Code, though elite splits and the 1857 panic slightly limited further gains. Civil society strengthened from a low base as merchants, artisans, newspapers, liberals, and Church-aligned opposition organized more visibly, but repression of the Sociedad de la Igualdad and defeated uprisings kept autonomous society below moderate strength.","society_change":0.7,"society_power":4.6,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":7.7}
